item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sterile FOundation members assigned to this location SCP: "not all of use were sterile BEFORE we were assigned here, ya know."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. Firstly, I just love this kind of unabashedly goofy stuff, as well as the questions it raises. Why do these anti-testicle ghosts manifest in this building in particular? Is their manifestation caused on purpose? I can’t even begin to imagine the conversation that led to this.

Dog Shelter Employee: Goddamn, I hate neutering dogs! It’s messy and shit just gets everywhere! I wish I had something to make it easier.

Wizard Fella who’s bored out of his mind and has nothing else to do: Make it easier, you say?

My one complaint, if anything, is that I want to see pictures of the testicle ghosts (not just the dog one). Alternatively, a plant that has been deemed to have “less testicles than before” despite just looking like a normal plant. It’s a missed opportunity.

Definitely a great entry into JamCon. There will be Greek epics about this skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
while not my top Shaggy Dog SCP, it's difinitely the one I had most fun with, +1
Still, I am disappointed that "testicles" isn't a tag :"(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As opposed to three eyes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is dumb, but in the most fantastic way. The way where the Foundation gets stuck with something absolutely ridiculous and just has to sigh and deal with this absurd but dangerous anomaly. And I have a special affection for conceptual anomalies. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the ball +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay! Thank you kindly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Consider it shrunk'd!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author, the image is over 1MB. Any chance you can shrink it down? Feel free to poke me if you would like assistance with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i had to read this with my two eyes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do think that could have used some form of example —
it's a good opportunity to provide some kind of odd or audacious visual (saying what something presents as visually in a way that one can't really envision is really counterintuitive).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no balls and I must scream.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You seem to be projecting an awful lot here on what an SCP should or shouldn't be; there are a great many successful articles on concepts that have no physical description, or things that defy physical description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know what this means and believe that this is because this phrase has no meaning.

At the risk of sounding curt (and maybe borderline defensive), just because you don't understand the meaning of a phrase doesn't make it meaningless. There's a lot of valid criticisms of this article — I wrote it in a day, after all — but I'm not sure "I don't know what this means and so I'm going to assume you intended it to have no meaning" is one of them. It seems more than a little presumptuous.

If the most, and/or only, visually arresting and interesting concept in the piece has no visual description, you have missed the mark.

yeah well thats just like your opinion man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say this is definitely one of my top 10 favorite SCP articles about dog testicles. Maybe top 15.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"… they are visually identifiable only as physical manifestations of the abstract notion of the removal of testicles."

I don't know what this means and believe that this is because this phrase has no meaning.

If the most, and/or only, visually arresting and interesting concept in the piece has no visual description, you have missed the mark.

-1, from what may have been a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"indefinably have fewer than before" is one of my favorite lines in a long time.

Thanks for sharing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know I'm a sucker for metaphysics, but aside from that this is delightfully weird, and not too long to wear out it's welcome.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where are my testicles, Summer?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said when I read the draft, this is great and I suspect it will do very well. +1

item.title: b o l l o c k s
item.content:encoded:
I don't know where this came from. Sorry. It was written for the 2019 JamCon, with the theme of "Shaggy Dog". I guess it fits well enough.

Thanks to Mortos, ManyMeats, PopcornK, and Uncle Nicolini for looking it over before I posted, and thanks to my dog Lucy for posing for the photograph. Hopefully having a female dog as an SCP-4234 instance doesn't break suspension of disbelief too hard :P
